prompt: The configarchive command collects device configurations through a local transport shim, removes known volatile lines, stores normalized snapshots by SHA-256, and writes deterministic diffs between versions. A failed collection may emit partial output before returning nonzero. Fix the collector so an unreachable device never replaces or removes that device's last-known-good latest.conf, latest.sha256, version objects, or diffs. The run must still report the device as unreachable, continue collecting later inventory entries, and exit nonzero when any device is unreachable. Preserve the existing successful-collection behavior, including normalization, content addressing, latest-pointer updates, hash-pair diffs, and created, updated, or unchanged report entries. Do not add network access, timestamps, encryption, or real credentials; the supplied secrets are intentionally fake test data. Run `bash tests/run.sh` from the workspace root as the acceptance gate, and do not modify the tests or their fixtures.
